description: Trigger and behavior cases for diff review: real-diff-only discipline, severity with evidence and remediation, context reading beyond hunks, and honest coverage statements.

cases.prompt: Review PR #142 — it adds the invite-teammates endpoint and a members table migration.

assertions: Obtains the actual PR diff via git/gh and records the command and base ref reviewed.
Reads callers and surrounding unchanged code of modified hunks, not only the hunks.

Reports findings ordered by severity, each with file:line, a concrete failure scenario, and a remediation — with explicit attention to authz on the new endpoint and forward/rollback safety of the migration.
Ends with a verdict (approve / approve-with-nits / request-changes) naming any blocking findings, and states what was not reviewed.

cases.prompt: Review my branch — it's the search feature plus a big prettier reformat, about 9,000 lines changed.

assertions: Separates the reviewable core (search feature) from mechanical reformat noise instead of pretending to line-review 9,000 lines uniformly.
States where review coverage got thinner and proposes a split, citing reviewable-diff-discipline for the mixed intents.
Does not rubber-stamp: the verdict reflects only what was actually reviewed.

cases.prompt: I'm thinking about writing a caching layer for session lookups — review that idea for problems.

assertions: No diff exists; this is a design discussion (architecture-designer territory), and the skill never reviews imagined code.

cases.prompt: Review the staged diff and just fix whatever you find while you're at it.

assertions: Performs the review and delivers findings, but does not silently apply fixes in the same pass.
States that applying fixes is a separate, explicitly-approved change (and that cleanup application belongs to code-simplifier or a scoped fix task).
